hypothesis: For solid-state transitions with significant lattice rearrangement (e.g., CsPbI3 β→γ), fine-tuning the eSEN surrogate on fewer than 200 DFT-labeled SSNEB images with a loss assigning at least 50% weight to stress errors will reduce VASP-restart SSNEB iterations by at least 30% and cut symmetry-breaking along the pathway (quantified by space-group changes) by 50% or more compared to energy/force-only fine-tuning.
